items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes (CVE-2026-53787) allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento (PHP object injection)
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-45247) in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This Adobe security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Security Update Released – APSB26-49
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-49, a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, bypassing validation and potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (APSB26-05)
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-05, a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.
